summary: RETEST of iteration_11 slowapi `response: Response` parameter fix. Re-ran the 2 originally-failing tests + smoke-tested 3 sibling endpoints that received the same fix. ALL 5 CHECKS PASS. No HTTP 500s on any endpoint. User-enumeration prevention verified (forgot-password returns identical body for known/unknown emails). Rate-limit decorator now coexists cleanly with success path on every endpoint that has `@limiter.limit`.

verification_results.test_forgot_password_known_email: PASS (HTTP 200, body='If email exists, reset link has been sent')
verification_results.test_forgot_password_unknown_email: PASS (HTTP 200, identical body — no user enumeration)
verification_results.smoke_reset_password_invalid_token: PASS (HTTP 400 'Invalid or expired reset token', not 500)

critical_code_review_comments: Fix verified — all 4 slowapi rate-limited endpoints (forgot-password, reset-password, public_widget_lead, razorpay_webhook) now correctly declare `response: Response`. Suggest adding a small pre-commit/CI assertion or wrapper that fails if any handler decorated with `@limiter.limit` lacks `response: Response` to prevent regression.

Carry-over from iteration_11 (not blocking): server.py is 5357 lines — split into routers/. Impersonation audit log lacks `ended_at`. No second-org user in seed (TestAssignLeadAuthorization cross-tenant test still skipped, but not in scope this iteration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: slowapi fix is fully verified. Next full-suite regression can simply run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_security.py (expect 26/26 pass + 1 skip for second-org). For valid-HMAC razorpay smoke test, compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body with RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET from backend/.env and send as X-Razorpay-Signature header — endpoint returns 200 with {handled:false} when the referenced subscription id does not exist (expected).
rca_of_the_issue: Already resolved in iteration_12 by main agent. RCA from iteration_11: slowapi Limiter initialised with headers_enabled=True requires every rate-limited handler to declare `response: Response` so it can inject X-RateLimit-* headers. Missing parameter caused HTTP 500 on the success branch (429 branch was unaffected). Fix: added `response: Response` to forgot_password (L1098), reset_password (L1123), razorpay_webhook (L3065), public_widget_lead (L4697). All four signatures verified via grep + functional pytest + curl.
